skill.name: close-automation
skill.description: Automate Close CRM operations through Composio's Close toolkit via Rube MCP including lead management, call logging, SMS, tasks, and notes.
skill.summary: Automate Close CRM tasks via Rube MCP for lead creation, call and SMS management, task tracking, and note handling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation for Close CRM automation via Rube MCP (Composio). Static analysis detected 42 external_commands patterns and 1 network pattern, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'backtick execution' patterns are Markdown code formatting (inline code blocks and code fences), not actual shell commands. The URL is a legitimate MCP server endpoint for configuration. No executable code exists in this file - it is purely instructional documentation for CRM operations.

content.user_title: Automate Close CRM Operations with AI
content.value_statement: Manual CRM data entry and activity logging consume valuable sales time. This skill automates lead creation, call logging, SMS management, and task tracking in Close CRM through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Create new leads with contacts and custom fields in Close CRM
Log outbound and inbound call activities with duration and status
Send and track SMS messages linked to leads and contacts
Create and manage follow-up tasks with due dates and assignments
Retrieve and manage notes associated with leads
Delete call activities and manage data integrity
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Close toolkit configured
Cannot auto-detect duplicate leads - manual verification required
Tasks require Close user IDs, not email addresses or names
SMS functionality requires Close phone integration to be configured

use_cases.title: Sales Representative Daily Workflow
use_cases.description: Automate post-call documentation by logging calls, creating follow-up tasks, and updating lead notes immediately after customer conversations.
use_cases.target_user: Sales representatives managing high call volumes

use_cases.title: Lead Intake Automation
use_cases.description: Streamline new lead creation from web forms or events by automatically creating leads with associated contacts and initial tasks.
use_cases.target_user: Sales operations managers handling lead imports

use_cases.title: Multi-Channel Outreach Tracking
use_cases.description: Coordinate and log all customer touchpoints including calls, SMS messages, and tasks in a unified CRM timeline.
use_cases.target_user: Account executives managing complex deal cycles

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new lead in Close CRM for [Company Name] with contact [Contact Name] at [Email] and [Phone]. Set the status to [Status] and add a note about [Context].

prompt_templates.title: Log a Customer Call
prompt_templates.prompt: Log an [outbound/inbound] call with [Lead Name] that lasted [X] minutes. The call status was [completed/no-answer/busy]. Add notes: [Call summary and next steps].

prompt_templates.title: Send Follow-up SMS
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an SMS to [Contact Name] at [Lead Name] with the following message: [Message content]. Mark it as outbound and link it to the lead record.

prompt_templates.title: Create Task Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a series of follow-up tasks for [Lead Name]: (1) Call in 2 days, (2) Send proposal in 5 days, (3) Check in after 1 week. Assign all tasks to [User] with appropriate due dates.

output_examples.input: Create a lead for Acme Corp with John Smith as contact
output_examples.output: Successfully created lead 'Acme Corp' (ID: lead_abc123) with contact John Smith (ID: cont_xyz789). Lead status set to New. Ready for follow-up activities.

output_examples.input: Log a 15-minute completed outbound call with Acme Corp
output_examples.output: Call activity logged successfully. Direction: outbound, Status: completed, Duration: 900 seconds. Associated with lead_abc123 and contact cont_xyz789. Timeline updated.

best_practices: Always search for existing leads before creating new ones to prevent duplicates
Verify Close connection status is ACTIVE before executing any CRM operations
Include relevant metadata (duration, direction, status) when logging activities for accurate reporting
anti_patterns: Creating leads without checking for existing records causes duplicate entries
Using email addresses instead of user IDs for task assignment will fail
Deleting activities without confirmation risks permanent data loss

faq.question: Do I need a Close API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No API key is required directly. You need an active Close connection via Rube MCP using RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the Close toolkit.

faq.question: What is the difference between leads and contacts in Close?
faq.answer: In Close, a Lead represents a company or organization. Contacts are individual people nested within a Lead. Activities like calls and SMS are linked to leads, with optional contact association.

faq.question: Can this skill send SMS messages automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, but your Close account must have phone/SMS integration configured. Outbound SMS may also require a verified sending number in your Close settings.

faq.question: How do I find the lead_id for an existing lead?
faq.answer: Use the Close search API to find leads by name or criteria. Extract the lead_id from results (format: lead_XXXXXXXXXXXXX) for use in activity creation.

faq.question: Can deleted activities be recovered?
faq.answer: No. Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. Only the activity creator or an admin can delete activities. Always confirm before deleting.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Close API rate limits?
faq.answer: Close returns 429 responses when rate limited. Implement delays between bulk operations and monitor response headers. Use exponential backoff when rate limited.
